questions.question_text: You want Claude to create a workout plan. Which opening line works better?

options.text: "Create a 30-minute workout plan for beginners"
"Do you know anything about exercise?"
"I was wondering about workouts and fitness stuff"
"What kind of workout should I do?"

questions.question_text: Claude keeps missing sarcastic comments when analyzing social media posts. What's the best way to fix this?

options.text: Ask it to guess when something might be sarcastic
Provide examples showing sarcastic posts labeled as negative
Tell it to "be more careful about sarcasm"
Make the prompt longer with more instructions
Training AI models on new datasets
Improving a prompt to get more reliable, higher-quality outputs
Programming AI models from scratch using code
Building the hardware infrastructure for AI systems

questions.question_text: "Providing sample input/output pairs to guide AI responses" describes which prompt engineering technique?

options.text: Being clear and direct
Iterative refinement
XML structuring
One-shot or multi-shot prompting

questions.question_text: What is the main purpose of using XML tags in prompts?

options.text: To reduce the token count of prompts
To increase the processing speed of AI models
To add structure and clarity, especially when including large amounts of content
To make prompts look more professional
